description: Begin at a lake's surface and follow the extent downward: that dimension is its 'nusho'. Bowls, wounds, shelves, and openings also have depth when the reference surface is understood or stated. 'nulo' (deep) and 'saphei' (shallow) judge that extent; 'raeli' (height) runs upward from a lower reference.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n' and carry the voice through the nose into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar area for unvoiced 'sh', send the air through the central groove, and finish with middle-height back rounded 'o'.
usage_notes: The thing being gauged comes first, as in 'thelomi nusho' (the lake's depth). An exact reference point, value, and unit remain with the measurement record when they matter.

semantic_domains.physical: inward or downward extent within a body, object, or opening
semantic_domains.quantity: a dimension available for comparison or measurement
semantic_domains.spatial: distance inward or downward from a reference surface
